app.transaction.failed.reason: Failed transaction activity can indicate attempted manipulation or fraud.

app.transaction.large.reason: Large transactions can represent business logic abuse or manipulation.

auth.login.failed.reason: Repeated authentication failures are commonly associated with brute force activity.

auth.login.success.reason: Successful authentication can indicate the use of valid accounts.

auth.password.reset.reason: Password reset activity can indicate account manipulation.

data.download.reason: Data download activity may represent collection from local systems.

data.report.export.reason: Report export activity may indicate automated data exfiltration.

data.transfer.large.reason: Large outbound data movement may indicate exfiltration.

detection.alert.reason: Generic detection alerts may be linked to discovery behavior depending on upstream logic.

device.usb.insert.reason: USB insertion can be linked to exfiltration over removable media.

file.access.reason: File access may indicate local collection.

network.connection.allowed.reason: Established network connections can support command and control or lateral movement.

network.scan.port.reason: Port scanning aligns with network service discovery.

privilege.change.reason: Privilege changes often indicate account manipulation.

privilege.escalation.reason: Privilege escalation behavior aligns with exploitation for privilege escalation.

process.execution.reason: Process execution may indicate use of a command or scripting interpreter.

process.execution.suspicious.reason: Suspicious process execution indicates likely command or scripting interpreter activity.

process.powershell.execution.reason: PowerShell execution maps directly to PowerShell interpreter abuse.

registry.modify.reason: Registry modification aligns with modify registry behavior.

user.activity.reason: General user activity may reflect valid-account behavior when paired with stronger evidence.
